description: After e-verification, ITR is processed by the Centralised Processing Centre (CPC), Bengaluru. Processing includes data validation, TDS matching, refund computation. Intimation u/s 143(1) is sent. Scrutiny assessment u/s 143(3) may follow for selected returns.
timeline: CPC processing: typically 15-90 days. Intimation u/s 143(1): within 9 months of end of AY (by December 31, 2027 for AY 2026-27).

steps.description: CPC validates: PAN, Aadhaar linkage, return schema, mathematical accuracy, TDS matching with Form 26AS/AIS, advance tax verification. Automated comparison with third-party information.

CPC sends intimation to registered email and on portal. Three outcomes: (a) Acceptance — return processed as filed; (b) Demand — additional tax payable due to CPC adjustments; (c) Refund — excess tax refunded.
steps.deadline: Within 9 months of end of AY (Dec 31, 2027 for AY 2026-27)

steps.description: If 143(1) shows demand: Pay via Challan 280 (self-assessment tax) within 30 days OR file rectification u/s 154 if demand is due to CPC error. Do not ignore demand — interest accrues from Assessment Order date.

CBDT's Computer Aided Scrutiny Selection (CASS) selects returns for detailed assessment u/s 143(2). Selection based on AI risk parameters: high income, large refunds, significant deductions, profession codes. Notice must be issued within 3 months of end of AY.
steps.deadline: Notice u/s 143(2): within 3 months of end of AY (June 30, 2027 for AY 2026-27)

steps.description: AO (Assessing Officer) examines return in detail. Sends questionnaires (u/s 142(1)). Taxpayer must respond with documents within specified time. AO passes assessment order with or without additions to income.
steps.deadline: Assessment order: within 12 months of end of AY (March 31, 2028 for AY 2026-27)

commonMistakes: Ignoring 143(1) demand notice — interest u/s 220(2) accrues at 1% per month
Not responding to 142(1) questionnaire within time — deemed income addition
Assuming 143(1) acceptance means no future scrutiny (selection for 143(2) can still happen)
consequences: Unaddressed 143(1) demand: Recovery proceedings u/s 220, penalty u/s 221
Non-response to 143(2): Best judgment assessment u/s 144
False statements in return: Prosecution u/s 276C
